title: Bookbot Outcomes, Repository Baseline, and Evidence · Language Clinic: Repository Baseline
summary: A volunteer digital library asks for a word-count script but has not defined the report user, authorized input, counting policy, failure behavior, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the repository baseline increment in Bookbot outcome, corpus, repository, and evidence c…
objectives: Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.
Record text owner, license or permission, source, version, checksum, language, and allowed use before analysis.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-outcomes-repo-evidence-3.
# Case 5798: a large-file migration rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain source permission, digest purpose, revision, Python, Unicode, policy, command, and output identity.
# Changed case: split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte.
def worked_bookbot_outcomes_repo_evidence_3(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "corpusworked3",
        "policy3dekrow",
        "revision5798",
        "commandworked35798",
        "report3dekrow5798",
        "test5798worked3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-repository-baseline-100lp3
# Competency: bookbot-repository-baseline.
# Case 5561: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: preserve original text and offsets beside every normalized comparison key and report exclusion.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def evidence_bookbot_repository_baseline_100lp3(user: str = "archive-reader", authorized: bool = True, acceptance_cases: int = 6, repository_clean: bool = True, changed_case_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_100lp3 = "100lp3-3pl001-5561"
    assert evidence_variant_100lp3
    evidence_axes_100lp3 = (
        "corpus100lp3",
        "policy3pl001",
        "revision5561",
        "command100lp35561",
        "report3pl0015561",
        "test5561100lp3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_100lp3)) == 6
    if not user.strip() or acceptance_cases < 1:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "corpus-authority"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not changed_case_kept:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "evidence-gap"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 1
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-charter"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Repository Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 7907 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the Bookbot outcome on user need, corpus authority, repository state, and changed-case evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect Git status, branch, remotes, configuration origin, tracked files, ignore rules, and baseline tests before editing.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A clean-looking editor proves the repository has no uncommitted work.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-repository-baseline-100lp3.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-repository-baseline-100lp3(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_repository_baseline_100lp3\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not user\.strip)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not authorized)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not repository_clean)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not changed_case_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
